textByHash.0149ce53cfea12131a6a38d1d714d8e5df08eaf6: Not many people can say they've met a Mahjarrat. Some don't even believe they exist.
textByHash.052e4406613903639165f1e4612649a01dccceab: And quite the glut of information it was. So I suppose you intend to try and discover what happened to these four individuals?
textByHash.08b567bfdbdf9050cf22dac7238f996d48e0b720: What is it with you and destroying things?
textByHash.097bd3cafbe6bb984a0c29f7683102f937315d05: Well... in my travels, I ended up spending a great deal of time in one of the few places Zarosian remnants can still be found. My time studying them filled me with fascination, and sadness.
textByHash.0b72967b3e19aeb31726d5a3a4cd4a13e37ae499: Excellent work. Shall we head in?
textByHash.136d6d7881598a417a3937a0b447875ca21975b6: It mentioned this Duke taking informal command. Seems he was quite important.
textByHash.13ebeeaeed77f99e5d33bbf89b4f488e99f3d56a: The Abyss? I've only ever heard legends of the Abyss.
textByHash.143deb803a4a9ede9b50c93a2bc67f8a9390e206: Of course, they wouldn't have wanted that information falling into the wrong hands, so recovering it may not be straightforward...
textByHash.1629e6a0d84ca76f74eb36e65fe4c457233b86bc: My apologies. I was not expecting company down here, so you caught me a bit off guard.
textByHash.208c3c14be6e23d90395b367fcd7932cd4a9aaa0: I've never been myself, but I do remember one of the other archaeologists, Elissa I think is her name, saying she'd visited Lovakengj.
textByHash.2712fa31602aa28cd6900f0b81b29c7535cbe416: I appreciate your concern for my safety. Very well, I will await your call.
textByHash.30184db0eab33935b29b20060dcf952bfbef13c9: Ah, of course. We know that Saradominists built a new city over the top of Senntisten. It's quite possible the dwarves did the same at Lassar.
textByHash.3217b1d566a95c7d1ef255154519911048a95c88: I guess you should do as it asks.
textByHash.340791b50895bee68762ccc3c5672fae733f218a: It's an old poem about the rise, and inevitable fall, of kingdoms and empires. Once, Zaros' Empire stretched as far as the eye could see. Now, no one even remembers his name.
textByHash.35cb2865626ef5874a32ab020b8e65e36ccc87f1: You seem to be fairly knowledgeable when it comes to the Zarosian Empire, which is quite a rarity these days. How much do you know about its fall?
textByHash.3b958f5cb7649812eda61b66ed5561f7ba30a9b5: Glad to hear it. Now, I have made good progress in excavating these ruins so far, but perhaps you can help me with the final step?
textByHash.3eedc65959c5423cb29b8ea41f14eab0330e1167: It mentioned Lovakengj, presumably as in the Great Kourend Lovakengj.
textByHash.403011e1cc427050f5b32cabb7a05d0a02dfdc28: I think so. I suspect the information you seek can be found in the very place I'm searching for.
textByHash.40edc78ea0b6df29162ae14eca83a391ed5d79e3: It mentioned Lassar, one of the old Zarosian fortresses, built atop Ice Mountain.
textByHash.4371f97ad8faab3904fa6f774e5801ce6307c9e0: Battle for the Eye? I've never heard of it.
textByHash.4499e164e14636a1e9de879e1e88c06de0aeca81: Where did you hear these names?
textByHash.4ae135a7d38387b02d3b534574f14b75b10be0c7: However, even to those of us who do know our stuff, much of what happened to the empire following Zaros' defeat is still a mystery. That's why I'm here.
textByHash.50ff36418baf33f1d1d0ed149708fd40583b93b8: If my research is right, and I have strong reason to believe it is, the remains of a key military installation are located right beneath us.
textByHash.57594952f81428d160726b47004945f5aa293d43: 'I walked across the sands and saw the ruins of time...'
textByHash.5a68de956987c8ea3d3abc4af4008a79b3624926: As I said, I hope to find details about the fall of the Zarosian Empire.
textByHash.6175ccc282edb0a35d93e72912a1d949f650cc75: Absolutely! Any respectable military outpost would have been filled to the brim with key strategic intelligence. I'd expect details on notable Zarosians to be included.
textByHash.63ccc8322fe107313910efda5e0aea0d1434712c: No, I imagine not. Still, from what she was saying, it sounded like she did spend time researching the history of the local area.
textByHash.68afc96a7190d70f33d1f8a9bae4781a15b076ec: How about we take just a touch more care with this one?
textByHash.7406107c9d7eb08664f5e2774849f3cbe7f39412: It was supposedly defined by its beautiful shining spires...
textByHash.75702c3637d9358d0d9a1db9777ba18960935550: I'd appreciate that. Anyway, time to see if this golem has what I'm after as well...
textByHash.7a252921375ea34bbd832017c964a50b18cfa40d: Grab yourself a pickaxe and carefully clear away these rocks. There should be a pickaxe in the crate in the corner.
textByHash.7a94e1df31100cc4dd935852a43041763f2822ec: I can definitely think of worse places to discover details on the fall of the empire.
textByHash.7d2eeff869f5e6cbca7ecba58b16b85504cf8faa: This place is remarkable! Let's have a look around and see what we can find.
textByHash.7e9878637c4e43c218a4aeaddf6575073171dff0: Well let's try and keep the destruction to a minimum from now on. If we're to discover anything down here, we'll need some things still intact.
textByHash.8231e25a4a803d033be27709644c71e795164d2c: Yes and no. It's not the definitive information I was hoping for, but at least it rules out some options.
textByHash.914585262ad09bb2e0dec20aa027952b69394e97: Lassar... One of the old Zarosian fortresses, built atop Ice Mountain.
textByHash.9d2b865ae1f81d74e0b273e73f314af4aa03eacf: Informal command? Seems this Duke was quite important.
textByHash.a190b625a87909f3b59c2022373d304a113b29ba: Spot on! You could teach the so-called experts around here a thing or two. The betrayal of Zaros ultimately led to the God Wars, making it quite possibly the most pivotal moment in all of history.
textByHash.a3d4f6e0e3f0e68d38d8bba6b5e8a0d3c8ed0f8f: Let's see if this golem has what I'm after as well.
textByHash.a633fccd59e0c976238d8bb0a34e3bf01adbfe38: Golems do have rudimentary intelligence. Maybe we can get some information out of it.
textByHash.a7c4c43ba70bca21a32e0918b592bc4e47b55bf9: Is it safe for me to head below?
textByHash.a815a031c1826ada949adc3ff5ebbc8632a2a0a4: I need to consult with a good friend of mine on this. Forgive me, but he lives quite far from here, so best I don't delay.
textByHash.abb11972b9c69b250e9921311a0551426874f41d: Absolutely! You have an opportunity here to uncover some lost history of the Zarosian Empire. You can't say no to that.
textByHash.af6df9aa8c39d416f325933b04dd5aca48ab9e67: You risked the structural integrity of an ancient temple by blowing it open with explosives?!
textByHash.b07fb10d1d0d1fcde8fc23780f3a4f16721dd300: So what was all that noise I heard?
textByHash.b4a935acdb6b91fe47d5a81c4025272e4b90fc3e: We need to find a way to get that golem working.
textByHash.bde3b649dc7028978435d20da0df3cdaf3db4fa5: I have a few leads. Details on individuals and artefacts that may have played a key role in the final days of the Empire.
textByHash.c18d9d0e78e3e65e5cec819306534963025eccae: A golem? Fascinating... Is this like the one that was defending this place?
textByHash.c5f876eb4e54ec40f124363a0f98cb0187023808: No. It seems to have been heavily modified in some way. I wonder...
textByHash.ca7ed2a0e0b2422a0ca3648dc0d0b0c5d22c4040: A few of the other archaeologists recently set up a dig in Camdozaal. Perhaps they'll have found something of note.
textByHash.cee57060e8731da64f3e9d2de08cc1e6556d1f44: Thank you for your help, adventurer. I hope we meet again soon.
textByHash.d0f142e6f083a7b27c0753a0939376a13d7e6277: Senntisten, the Zarosian settlement once located here, was believed to be the capital of the empire.
textByHash.e4542fa8903e02ef48d2c786d1392166c5bfec87: So tell me, adventurer, what brings you down here?
textByHash.e75895f466f78cf580d53d5399e8d7349911662b: I'd say it's definitely possible. I suppose there's only one way to find out.
textByHash.edee19685d321ff5996402552724fa36b6c8dc0c: It may be of little use in its current state. It doesn't seem to be giving off any magical energy.
textByHash.f03f9183ea8d5aadc01e1d4f6b8a678992a08e11: And on that note, we should take a look around.
textByHash.f0c450021e86fd6be5a474dc09a19be49b1c4e38: Lovakengj? As in the Great Kourend Lovakengj?
textByHash.f3187d0a044ea91392631cc9bda4c364e124bd99: To think that something that was once so absolute may soon be lost forever... It falls to people like me, and perhaps you as well, to ensure that history isn't forgotten.
textByHash.fc77f3bbcdd40eb18a42d90cc4c9b27731b0756f: I did. You must forgive me, friend, for I'm afraid I've deceived you in another way as well.
